page_content:
BULM756 Supply Chain Strategy (2 Credits) Examines supply chain management at the strategic level. Through class lectures, case discussions, and assignments, students ﬁrst learn about the determinants of corporate strategy. Supply chain strategies are then discussed, along with the need to properly align supply chain strategy with corporate strategy. Finally, there is a discussion of how to implement supply chain strategy, given various environmental and market considerations. Restriction: Must be in Business and Management (Master's) program; or permission of BMGT-Robert H. Smith School of Business. Credit Only Granted for: BULM758B or BULM756. Formerly: BULM758B.

BULM758 Special Topics in Logistics, Business and Public Policy (1-4 Credits) Selected advanced topics in the various ﬁelds of graduate study in logistics, business and public policy. Prerequisite: Permission of BMGT-Robert H. Smith School of Business. Repeatable to: 9 credits if content differs. Formerly: BMGT798.

BULM759 Independent Study in Logistics Management (1-6 Credits) Independent study for Masters students in Logistics Management. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs. Formerly: BMGT708.
